  "findings": [
    "Soft foot condition remains uncorrected during shaft alignment",
    "Dial indicator reading exceeds allowable tolerance limits"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Loosen hold-down bolts and measure individual foot deflection using dial indicators or laser alignment system",
    "Add precision shims under the affected foot to eliminate the soft foot condition",
    "Re-torque hold-down bolts to specification and re-verify alignment"
  ],
  "rationale": "A soft foot condition introduces frame distortion and high vibration when hold-down bolts are torqued, making a stable and precise shaft alignment impossible. According to API 686, soft foot must be corrected to within 0.05 mm (0.002 inches) before final shaft alignment can proceed.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 686 Chapter 7",
    "Laser Alignment System Manuals"
  ]
}
